visual: A four-part sequence on the left side: ward off, rollback, press, and push, executed in a smooth forward-and-back bow stance transition.
motion_description: The sequence begins by stepping out with the left foot and shifting weight forward into a left bow stance, as the left forearm sweeps upward to chest height and the right hand drops low. The torso then rotates to the left, drawing both arms backward and downward in a continuous, flowing diagonal arc while the weight shifts entirely onto the right leg. Next, the right palm connects to the left inner forearm, and both arms press forward as the body turns to the front and weight shifts to the left leg. Finally, the hands separate shoulder-width apart, the weight transfers back to the right leg, and both palms push forward at chest height as the weight returns to the left stance.
